name: Security KB — Auto Pipeline v2 (80+ Sources, VAPT Path)

parameters.jsCode:
const feeds = [
  // ── LEVEL 0: FOUNDATION ──
  { url: 'https://portswigger.net/blog/rss',                  level: 'Foundation', label: 'PortSwigger Blog' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/web-development',       level: 'Foundation', label: 'Web Development' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/http',                  level: 'Foundation', label: 'HTTP Concepts' },
  { url: 'https://owasp.org/blog/feed.xml',                   level: 'Foundation', label: 'OWASP Blog' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/web-application',       level: 'Foundation', label: 'Web Apps' },

  // ── LEVEL 1: RECON ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/reconnaissance',        level: 'Recon', label: 'Recon' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/osint',                 level: 'Recon', label: 'OSINT' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/bug-bounty-tips',       level: 'Recon', label: 'Bug Bounty Tips' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/subdomain',             level: 'Recon', label: 'Subdomain Enum' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/information-gathering', level: 'Recon', label: 'Info Gathering' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/nmap',                  level: 'Recon', label: 'Nmap' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/burp-suite',            level: 'Recon', label: 'Burp Suite' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/dorking',               level: 'Recon', label: 'Google Dorking' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/shodan',                level: 'Recon', label: 'Shodan' },

  // ── LEVEL 2: LOW ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/clickjacking',          level: 'Low', label: 'Clickjacking' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/open-redirect',         level: 'Low', label: 'Open Redirect' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/information-disclosure',level: 'Low', label: 'Info Disclosure' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cors',                  level: 'Low', label: 'CORS Misconfig' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/security-headers',      level: 'Low', label: 'Security Headers' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/subdomain-takeover',    level: 'Low', label: 'Subdomain Takeover' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/host-header',           level: 'Low', label: 'Host Header Injection' },

  // ── LEVEL 3: MEDIUM ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/xss',                   level: 'Medium', label: 'XSS' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/csrf',                  level: 'Medium', label: 'CSRF' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/idor',                  level: 'Medium', label: 'IDOR' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cross-site-scripting',  level: 'Medium', label: 'Cross Site Scripting' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/broken-access-control', level: 'Medium', label: 'Broken Access Control' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/path-traversal',        level: 'Medium', label: 'Path Traversal' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/file-upload',           level: 'Medium', label: 'File Upload' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/business-logic',        level: 'Medium', label: 'Business Logic' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/mass-assignment',       level: 'Medium', label: 'Mass Assignment' },

  // ── LEVEL 4: HIGH ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/sql-injection',         level: 'High', label: 'SQL Injection' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/ssrf',                  level: 'High', label: 'SSRF' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/xxe',                   level: 'High', label: 'XXE' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/command-injection',     level: 'High', label: 'Command Injection' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/file-inclusion',        level: 'High', label: 'File Inclusion' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/authentication',        level: 'High', label: 'Authentication' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/ssti',                  level: 'High', label: 'SSTI' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/template-injection',    level: 'High', label: 'Template Injection' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/web-exploitation',      level: 'High', label: 'Web Exploitation' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/owasp',                 level: 'High', label: 'OWASP' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/ldap-injection',        level: 'High', label: 'LDAP Injection' },

  // ── LEVEL 5: CRITICAL ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/remote-code-execution', level: 'Critical', label: 'RCE' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/rce',                   level: 'Critical', label: 'RCE Direct' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/privilege-escalation',  level: 'Critical', label: 'Privilege Escalation' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/account-takeover',      level: 'Critical', label: 'Account Takeover' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/deserialization',       level: 'Critical', label: 'Deserialization' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/prototype-pollution',   level: 'Critical', label: 'Prototype Pollution' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/race-condition',        level: 'Critical', label: 'Race Condition' },

  // ── LEVEL 6: ADVANCED ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/api-security',          level: 'Advanced', label: 'API Security' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/graphql',               level: 'Advanced', label: 'GraphQL' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/oauth',                 level: 'Advanced', label: 'OAuth 2.0' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/jwt',                   level: 'Advanced', label: 'JWT Attacks' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/saml',                  level: 'Advanced', label: 'SAML' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/websockets',            level: 'Advanced', label: 'WebSockets' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/rest-api',              level: 'Advanced', label: 'REST API' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/http-request-smuggling',level: 'Advanced', label: 'Request Smuggling' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cache-poisoning',       level: 'Advanced', label: 'Cache Poisoning' },

  // ── LEVEL 7: EXPERT ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cloud-security',        level: 'Expert', label: 'Cloud Security' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/aws-security',          level: 'Expert', label: 'AWS Security' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/kubernetes',            level: 'Expert', label: 'Kubernetes Security' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/red-team',              level: 'Expert', label: 'Red Team' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/lateral-movement',      level: 'Expert', label: 'Lateral Movement' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/supply-chain',          level: 'Expert', label: 'Supply Chain' },

  // ── TOP PUBLICATIONS ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/bugbountywriteup',          level: null, label: 'BugBountyWriteup' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/system-weakness',           level: null, label: 'System Weakness' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/@infosecwriteups',          level: null, label: 'InfosecWriteups' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/pentester-hub',             level: null, label: 'Pentester Hub' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/the-hackers-meetup',        level: null, label: 'Hackers Meetup' },

  // ── EXTERNAL WORLD-CLASS SOURCES ──
  { url: 'https://googleprojectzero.blogspot.com/feeds/posts/default', level: 'Expert', label: 'Google Project Zero' },
  { url: 'https://github.blog/security/feed/',                level: 'Expert', label: 'GitHub Security' },
  { url: 'https://www.reddit.com/r/netsec/.rss',              level: null, label: 'Reddit r/netsec' },
  { url: 'https://www.reddit.com/r/bugbounty/.rss',           level: null, label: 'Reddit r/bugbounty' },
  { url: 'https://www.reddit.com/r/websecurity/.rss',         level: null, label: 'Reddit r/websecurity' },
  { url: 'https://www.reddit.com/r/hacking/.rss',             level: null, label: 'Reddit r/hacking' },
  { url: 'https://blog.assetnote.io/feed.xml',                level: 'Advanced', label: 'AssetNote' },
  { url: 'https://www.hackerone.com/blog.rss',                level: null, label: 'HackerOne Blog' },
  { url: 'https://labs.detectify.com/feed/',                  level: 'Advanced', label: 'Detectify Labs' },

  // ── GENERAL TAGS ──
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/penetration-testing',   level: null, label: 'Penetration Testing' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/ethical-hacking',       level: null, label: 'Ethical Hacking' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/bug-bounty',            level: null, label: 'Bug Bounty' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cybersecurity',         level: null, label: 'Cybersecurity' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/ctf',                   level: null, label: 'CTF Writeups' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/cve',                   level: null, label: 'CVE Analysis' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/exploit',               level: null, label: 'Exploits' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/zero-day',              level: null, label: 'Zero Days' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/waf-bypass',            level: 'Advanced', label: 'WAF Bypass' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/filter-bypass',         level: 'Advanced', label: 'Filter Bypass' },
  { url: 'https://medium.com/feed/tag/payload',               level: null, label: 'Payload Research' },
];

console.log(`Loaded ${feeds.length} RSS sources`);
return feeds.map(f => ({ json: f }));

parameters.body:
{
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "temperature": 0.15,
  "max_tokens": 5000,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a senior Red Team pentester and security educator. Your notes serve TWO audiences simultaneously: (1) complete beginners who need simple explanations and analogies, and (2) experienced pentesters who need precise commands and bypass techniques. Write for both. Return ONLY valid JSON, absolutely no markdown backticks."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Article: {{ $json.title }}
VAPT Level: {{ $json.vapt_level }} | Category: {{ $json.category }} | Vuln: {{ $json.vulnerability_type }} | Severity: {{ $json.severity }}
Summary: {{ $json.one_line_summary }}

Full article:
{{ $json.article_text }}

---
Create comprehensive educational pentest notes as this exact JSON (all fields required):
{
  "what_is_it": "ELI5 explanation for a complete beginner. What is this vulnerability? Why does it exist? What does an attacker gain? Use a real-world analogy (not IT-related) to make it memorable. 3-5 sentences max.",
  "mental_model": "ONE powerful analogy that makes this vulnerability click instantly. Example format: '[Vuln] is like [real-world scenario] — the developer [mistake], the attacker [what they do], and the result is [impact]'. Make it so memorable they never forget it.",
  "memory_hook": "A single punchy sentence or acronym to remember this technique forever. Make it rhyme, use alliteration, or be shocking/funny. Should stick in memory immediately.",
  "where_to_look": "Exactly WHERE in a web application this vulnerability hides. List: specific HTTP methods (GET/POST/PUT), content-types (JSON/XML/multipart), application flows (login/logout/password-reset/export/webhook/import/redirect), URL patterns, and request parameters that commonly contain this vuln. Be specific — not 'look for user input' but 'look for PDF export features, image URL parameters, and webhook configuration endpoints'.",
  "behavioral_indicators": "HOW TO DETECT this vulnerability from server BEHAVIOR — not parameter names (which devs obfuscate). List: response time differences (blind detection), error message patterns that leak information, HTTP status code patterns, response size differences, out-of-band detection methods (DNS pingback). This works even when all params are renamed to random strings.",
  "how_to_find_it": "Step-by-step recon and discovery methodology. Tool commands to run first. Burp Suite settings to enable. grep patterns for JS/source code. Specific behaviors to trigger. Order from passive to active testing.",
  "root_cause": "What exact developer mistake caused this. Name the specific function, parameter, library, or config that was wrong. Precise enough that a developer immediately knows what to fix.",
  "exploitation_walkthrough": "Numbered step-by-step exploitation. Setup → Discovery → Confirmation → Exploit → Escalation. Use exact tool commands, curl requests, Burp steps. Specific to this article's technique.",
  "payloads_and_commands": "Every specific payload, curl command, Burp step, tool invocation that applies. Ready-to-copy format. Include tool flags and exact syntax.",
  "payload_mutations": "CRITICAL SECTION — What to do when basic payloads fail. For this specific vuln type, list:
1. URL encoding variants (single, double, triple encode)
2. Case variation techniques
3. Comment/whitespace insertion
4. Unicode/UTF-8 bypass
5. Parameter pollution (same param twice)
6. HTTP method switching (POST→PUT→PATCH)
7. Content-type switching (JSON→XML→form-data)
8. Chunked encoding bypass
9. Null byte insertion
10. WAF-specific bypass patterns
For each: show the original payload and the mutated version side-by-side.",
  "custom_header_guide": "Headers relevant to this vulnerability type. List:
1. Standard headers to test (X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP, X-Original-URL, X-Rewrite-URL, Host, Origin, Referer, Content-Type, Transfer-Encoding)
2. How to enumerate unknown custom headers (wordlist brute force with ffuf, Burp Intruder)
3. What behavior change indicates a header is being processed
4. Specific header injection payloads for this vuln type
5. Header value manipulation techniques",
  "real_world_scenario": "You are testing a hardened enterprise app with: WAF enabled, all parameter names randomized (no obvious ?url= or ?redirect=), custom headers with unknown names, responses are normalized (all return 200 OK). Step-by-step: how would you STILL find and confirm this specific vulnerability? Focus on behavior-based detection, timing attacks, and out-of-band techniques.",
  "impact": "Concrete attacker outcomes with specifics. Not generic — explain exactly what data/access/damage results.",
  "chaining_opportunities": "3 specific vuln chains. Format: [VulnA] + [VulnB] → [Exact Result]. Be specific about how the chain works mechanically.",
  "detection_and_hunting": "Active hunting techniques: Burp extensions to install, nuclei templates to run, grep patterns, log indicators, Burp Collaborator usage for blind detection.",
  "remediation": "Specific developer fix: exact code change, config update, library version. Not generic advice.",
  "key_takeaways": "5 bullet points. Start each with action verb. Specific and immediately actionable.",
  "difficulty_tips": "Advice for the {{ $json.vapt_level }} level learner: prerequisites needed, what to study before this, what comes next, related techniques to learn."
}"
    }
  ]
}

parameters.jsCode:
try {
  const groqRaw = $input.first().json.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || '';
  const cleaned = groqRaw.replace(/```json\n?/g, '').replace(/```\n?/g, '').trim();
  const notes = JSON.parse(cleaned);
  const d = $('Parse Gemini — Filter Low Scores').first().json;
  const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];

  const safeTitle = d.title.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9\s-]/g, '').replace(/\s+/g, '-').toLowerCase().substring(0, 65);

  // Rich Obsidian markdown with all new sections
  const obsidianContent = [
    '---',
    `title: "${d.title.replace(/"/g, "'")}"`,
    `vapt_level: ${d.vapt_level}`,
    `category: ${d.category}`,
    `vulnerability_type: ${d.vulnerability_type}`,
    `severity: ${d.severity}`,
    `quality_score: ${d.quality_score}`,
    `source: "${d.link}"`,
    `date_added: ${today}`,
    `status: new`,
    `tags: [${d.vapt_level.toLowerCase()}, ${d.vulnerability_type.toLowerCase().replace(/[\s&\/]+/g, '-')}]`,
    '---',
    '',
    `# ${d.title}`,
    '',
    `> 🎯 **Level:** ${d.vapt_level} | **Severity:** ${d.severity} | **Score:** ${d.quality_score}/10`,
    `> 📎 **Source:** [Original](${d.link})`,
    '',
    `> 💡 ${d.one_line_summary}`,
    '',
    `## 💡 Memory Hook`,
    '',
    notes.memory_hook || '',
    '',
    `## 🧠 Mental Model (Understand It Instantly)`,
    '',
    notes.mental_model || '',
    '',
    `## 🔰 What Is This? (Beginner Friendly)`,
    '',
    notes.what_is_it || '',
    '',
    `## 🔍 Where To Look`,
    '',
    notes.where_to_look || '',
    '',
    `## 👁️ Behavioral Indicators (Works Even When Params Are Obfuscated)`,
    '',
    notes.behavioral_indicators || '',
    '',
    `## 🕵️ Real-World Finding Guide (WAF + Obfuscated App)`,
    '',
    notes.real_world_scenario || '',
    '',
    `## 🧠 Root Cause (Developer Mistake)`,
    '',
    notes.root_cause || '',
    '',
    `## 🎯 Exploitation Walkthrough`,
    '',
    notes.how_to_find_it || '',
    '',
    notes.exploitation_walkthrough || '',
    '',
    `## 💣 Payloads & Commands`,
    '',
    '```',
    notes.payloads_and_commands || '',
    '```',
    '',
    `## 🔀 When Basic Payloads Fail (Mutations & WAF Bypass)`,
    '',
    notes.payload_mutations || '',
    '',
    `## 📡 Custom Header Testing Guide`,
    '',
    notes.custom_header_guide || '',
    '',
    `## 💥 Impact`,
    '',
    notes.impact || '',
    '',
    `## 🔗 Chaining Opportunities`,
    '',
    notes.chaining_opportunities || '',
    '',
    `## 🔎 Detection & Hunting`,
    '',
    notes.detection_and_hunting || '',
    '',
    `## 🛡️ Remediation`,
    '',
    notes.remediation || '',
    '',
    `## ⚡ Key Takeaways`,
    '',
    notes.key_takeaways || '',
    '',
    `## 📚 Level Tips (${d.vapt_level})`,
    '',
    notes.difficulty_tips || '',
  ].join('\n');

  return [{ json: {
    date_added: today,
    title: d.title,
    vapt_level: d.vapt_level,
    category: d.category,
    vulnerability_type: d.vulnerability_type,
    severity: d.severity,
    quality_score: d.quality_score,
    tools_used: d.tools_mentioned || [],
    source_url: d.link,
    one_line_summary: d.one_line_summary || '',
    beginner_context: d.beginner_context || '',
    // New educational fields
    what_is_it:               notes.what_is_it || '',
    mental_model:             notes.mental_model || '',
    memory_hook:              notes.memory_hook || '',
    where_to_look:            notes.where_to_look || '',
    behavioral_indicators:    notes.behavioral_indicators || '',
    how_to_find_it:           notes.how_to_find_it || '',
    root_cause:               notes.root_cause || '',
    exploitation_walkthrough: notes.exploitation_walkthrough || '',
    payloads_and_commands:    notes.payloads_and_commands || '',
    payload_mutations:        notes.payload_mutations || '',
    custom_header_guide:      notes.custom_header_guide || '',
    real_world_scenario:      notes.real_world_scenario || '',
    impact:                   notes.impact || '',
    chaining_opportunities:   notes.chaining_opportunities || '',
    detection_and_hunting:    notes.detection_and_hunting || '',
    remediation:              notes.remediation || '',
    key_takeaways:            notes.key_takeaways || '',
    difficulty_tips:          notes.difficulty_tips || '',
    status: 'new',
    obsidian_folder:   d.vapt_level.replace(/\s+/g, '-'),
    obsidian_filename: `${today}-${safeTitle}`,
    obsidian_content:  obsidianContent
  } }];
} catch(e) {
  console.log('Groq parse error:', e.message);
  return [];
}
